summary: The v0.2 candidate now ships one checked composite instruction, exact TSC-compatible target validation, replayable self evidence, and an honest source-only package boundary. It remains a candidate because arbitrary methodology declaration loading, independent calibration, consistency sampling, admissibility, and held-out validation are not shipped.

positive: SKILL.md, README.md, the materialized INSTRUCTION.md, and its checked core/supplement assembly use one recursive L0-L4 vocabulary and one source-only path contract.
The exact v3.2.4 output field list and primary-axis rules are stable across schema, validator, composite instruction, and fixtures.
alpha.reason: The candidate presents one internally stable v0.2 instrument and explicitly separates executable manual composition from unshipped declaration loading.

positive: validate-tsc-targets.sh mirrors the pinned registry/manifest parser and prefix/suffix expansion, checks exact registrations, paths, names, manifest kinds, and non-empty historical bundles.
Eight negative fixtures cover missing registry/instruction/preflight, preflight mode, missing/malformed/name-mismatched manifests, and empty bundles.
assemble-instruction.sh binds the exact pinned TSC core, cnos supplement, and composite digests before the CLI consumes the single instruction file.
negative: TSC still cannot load the frontmatter methodology declaration itself; the executable route is explicitly manual and source-checkout-only.
beta.reason: All claimed source-route relations are executable and refusal-tested, while the remaining cross-repository loader edge is bounded instead of claimed.

positive: The self manifest fixes ordered inputs and excludes output receipts, avoiding circular measurement.
The package smoke proves shipped layout and installed activation refusal, and the historical preflight pins both target revision and instruction bytes.
All standing language remains zero: same-author diagnostic evidence cannot confer consistency, admissibility, independent, or held-out standing.
negative: No shipped TSC migration yet replaces the source-only/manual route with arbitrary methodology declaration loading.
Independent calibration, three-sample semantic consistency, admissibility measurement, and held-out validation remain future work without completed evidence.
gamma.reason: The candidate has strong fail-closed evolution boundaries, but promotion and generic executor migration remain deliberately incomplete, making process coherence the bottleneck.

defect_cards.evidence: SKILL.md section 0 and README.md exact route
defect_cards.summary: The generic TSC executor does not load the arbitrary methodology declaration; v0.2 requires the bounded manual composite route.

defect_cards.evidence: SKILL.md status prerequisites and calibration commons
defect_cards.summary: Independent calibration, semantic consistency, admissibility, and held-out standing are required but not completed.
No released TSC migration supports separate installed authority and target roots or first-class declaration loading.

next_fixes.fix: Implement and version TSC arbitrary methodology loading with distinct authority-root and target-root semantics.
Run three independent frozen-prompt samples, public calibration cases, admissibility measurement, and a held-out cnos target before changing standing.
